title: Botox for migraines - does it work？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Botox for migraines - does it work？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So botox for migraines does it work [Music] well the answer is yes there is data to support that it will improve a proportion of people who have chronic migraine and that's a key aspect to this it helps people with chronic migraine there's actually no license to treat people who …

segments.text: so botox for migraines does it work [Music] well the answer is yes there is data to support that it will improve a proportion of people who have chronic migraine and that's a key aspect to this it helps people with chronic migraine there's actually no license to treat people who only have a migraine once or twice a month so these are people who have a migraine almost every other day um they have a there's a proportion of them who get a really great difference and their migraines will stay away so long as the toxin is active now unfortunately within that cohort there are also proportions who don't get much difference at all so it's one of those that if it works it really works but there's a proportion of people who get very little difference at all so you need to consent your patients for this which no doubt you will be if you're doing this treatment and then you need to talk it through to them and make sure it's worth their while and consult them and prepare them for the fact that in some people it just doesn't make a great difference but there is good data to show that it makes a difference for some people

transcript: so botox for migraines does it work [Music] well the answer is yes there is data to support that it will improve a proportion of people who have chronic migraine and that's a key aspect to this it helps people with chronic migraine there's actually no license to treat people who only have a migraine once or twice a month so these are people who have a migraine almost every other day um they have a there's a proportion of them who get a really great difference and their migraines will stay away so long as the toxin is active now unfortunately within that cohort there are also proportions who don't get much difference at all so it's one of those that if it works it really works but there's a proportion of people who get very little difference at all so you need to consent your patients for this which no doubt you will be if you're doing this treatment and then you need to talk it through to them and make sure it's worth their while and consult them and prepare them for the fact that in some people it just doesn't make a great difference but there is good data to show that it makes a difference for some people
